title: Pickle's pediatrician follow-up — what to ask about the rash
summary: Notes from yesterday's call. Three questions to ask at Tuesday's appointment, and a list of foods to track this week.
body_html:
Pediatrician follow-up notes

Brief summary of yesterday's nurse call about the rash on Pickle's left forearm and the upcoming Tuesday in-person visit.

Questions for Dr. Naaz on Tuesday

  1. The rash flared up on Wednesday and faded by Saturday. Could it be a delayed reaction to something in the daycare snack rotation, or is the timing too long for a food cause?
  2. If the rash returns in the next 7 days, is there a topical we can use immediately or should we hold off until you see it?
  3. The nurse mentioned a referral to a pediatric allergist if it happens a third time. How long is the waitlist and should we get on it preemptively?

Food log to keep this week

The nurse recommended tracking everything Pickle eats for the next seven days, with the time and any reaction noted. Don't worry about portions — just the items and timing.

DayMealItemsNotes
MonBreakfast(fill in)
MonLunch(fill in)
MonSnack(fill in)
MonDinner(fill in)

I'll generate a fresh weekly version of this table every Sunday until we have a clearer picture or Dr. Naaz says we can stop.

Appointment details: Tuesday April 9 at 2:30pm, Lincoln Park Pediatrics, suite 240. Bring the food log, photos of the rash from Wednesday and Friday (they're in the family vault under health/pickle/rash-april-2026/), and Pickle's current immunization card.
